text: To our end, you can see the anterior hilum with fat overlying pulmonary vein, pericardium, PA, and aorta.
Lift the fat away from the vein and divide the fat with bipolar initially to develop a plane.
This view shows the phrenic nerve.
I create tension on the tissue with the left caudate, dissect the tissue away from the vein, and divide it with bipolar.
I attempt to create a plane behind the lingual branch without much success.
I remove the tissue on top of the pulmonary vein and top of the pulmonary artery.
This allows clear dissection of the exit point.
I then dissect the apical anterior branch.
The tissue around the vein is used to help get around the pulmonary vein.
Getting into the avascular plane around the vein allows safe dissection, gentle spreading to separate the vein away from the pulmonary artery and bronchus.
Once there's enough separation, I place the tip up instrument.
I get around the vein and dissect the tissue on top of the tip using the Cartier.
I then place a curved white robot stapler around the vane and divide the vane.
Then dissect the exit point for the lingular branch.
I place a tip up instrument around it.
and then divide it with robot wide-low stapler.
